mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears, causing institutional investors to de-risk equity portfolios and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power (F-stat p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term market panic and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months following an INDPRO decline, or the signal fails to predict returns during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers become prohibitively high, suppressing investment and consumption while forcing equity valuation multiples to contract.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a piecewise regression test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining total nonfarm payrolls combined with rising unemployment rates confirms a broad labor market deterioration, reducing household income and corporate profit expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor weakness signal yields positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals peak economic momentum, prompting equity markets to price in imminent monetary tightening and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with positive 20-day returns in >60% of late-cycle quarters, or the predictive power reverses sign during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy order books, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following positive INDPRO growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.
